$comment: D-LINK-LOCAL-FN-ADDR-STATIC-DATA-VA0: a file-LOCAL (`static`) global/function must NEVER be identified with a same-named EXTERNALLY-VISIBLE (Global) symbol in another CU (C 6.2.2p3 internal linkage). Distilled from the sqlite testfixture crash: os_unix.c's `static ... aSyscall[]` (rows -> the static posixOpen) shared the name `aSyscall` with test_syscall.c's NON-static `aSyscall[]` (rows -> ts_open); the cross-CU MIR merge FOLDED the Local onto the Global (assignSymbol) AND dropped the Local as a shadowed loser (isShadowedLoser), so os_unix's own code read test_syscall's table -> a call through a NULL fn-ptr at the first FILE-db open. Invisible until BOTH TUs link (the 2-TU sqlite3.c+main build lacked the extern aSyscall). Here os_unix.c's `static vfsSyscall` (-> localOpen, 42) collides with testvfs.c's extern `vfsSyscall` (-> testOpen, 999); main returns 42 IFF the two tables stayed distinct. RED-ON-DISABLE: restore the merge name-collapse -> osOpen reads testvfs's table -> returns 999 -> exit 231, not 42. Witnessed end-to-end on WSL glibc (elf x86_64) + qemu (elf aarch64) + the darwin CI leg (macho arm64) + natively on windows (pe64); every arm routes through the LK11 whole-program MIR merge, so ALL formats exercise the Local-vs-Global identity fix.
